description: No arity diagnostic (issue #111): the forms the static arity check must leave alone. `(print "x" 1)` and `(word "a" "b" "c")` are parenthesized primitive calls — the alternate/variadic escape hatch the single-number arity table cannot describe, so the check stays conservative and never flags a parenthesized primitive call. `g` has a required parameter and an optional (defaulted) one; the optional argument can only be supplied via the parenthesized form, so `(g 1 2)` is within [1, 2] and the bare `g 1` meets the required floor. All clean: an empty diagnostics stream.
